subject: Statistics
title: Random Assignment: The Gold Standard
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Random Assignment: The Gold Standard.

hook.text: Assign patients to treatment groups by flipping a coin. It sounds careless — it's actually genius.

buildup.text: Random assignment ensures that differences between groups are due to chance, not pre-existing differences.

discovery.text: It balances both known and unknown confounders. You don't need to know every variable — randomization handles it.

twist.text: Randomization doesn't guarantee balance in small samples. You can get unlucky. Blocking and stratification help.

climax.text: The randomized controlled trial became the gold standard of evidence because of this one elegant idea.

punchline.text: Let chance decide the groups. That way, only the treatment differs.

quiz.question: What is the main advantage of random assignment in experiments?
options: It balances both known and unknown confounding variables between groups
It eliminates the need for a control group
